description: Representative reliability task suite for the autonomous-dev pipeline. Tasks span the risk classes observed in production (trivial-docs, small, standard). Each task is a self-contained request the runner submits against a disposable scratch repo. sizeClass maps to the CLI `--size` flag. Keep this small: every live run is ~$3/~30min (see README).

tasks.description: Append a single line 'Maintained by the autonomous-dev reliability harness.' to the end of README.md. Do not modify any other file. If README.md does not exist, create it with exactly that one line.

Add a Python function add(a, b) that returns a + b in a module named calc.py (create it if absent), and add a pytest unit test in test_calc.py covering add(2, 3) == 5 and add(-1, 1) == 0. Keep any existing tests passing.

Refactor hello.py to define a function greet(name='world') that returns the string 'hello <name>' (e.g. greet() -> 'hello world', greet('ada') -> 'hello ada'), and make the __main__ block print greet(). Preserve existing behavior: running 'python hello.py' must still print exactly 'hello world'. Add pytest unit tests in test_hello.py for greet() covering both the default and a custom name, keeping the existing script-output test passing.
